title: Contributing to LAVA
text: outlines propose change LAVA.

title: Contributing to LAVA
text: can fix typos, spelling mistakes, grammatical errors documentation directly using GitHub web interface, long changes made source file.

title: Contributing to LAVA
text: want make bigger change, ’s good idea first file issue make sure someone team agrees ’s needed. ’ve found bug, please file issue illustrates bug minimal reprex (also help write unit test, needed).

title: Contributing to LAVA
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("isadoo/LAVA", fork = TRUE). Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR usethis::pr_push(). PR include title, description, check package ’re ready PR merged yet, convert draft PR.

title: Contributing to LAVA
text: New code follow tidyverse style guide. Make sure run styler::style_pkg() submitting PR. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases easier accept.

title: Contributing to LAVA
text: Please note LAVA project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project agree abide terms.

title: LAVA Development Guide
text: LAVA package follows standard R package structure: R/: Contains R code man/: Documentation (generated roxygen2) tests/: Unit tests vignettes/: Package vignettes data/: Package data data-raw/: Scripts generate package data

title: LAVA Development Guide
text: Documentation generated using roxygen2. Make sure document exported functions roxygen comments: Generate documentation using:
code: #' Function Title #'  #' @param param1 Description of parameter 1 #' @return Description of return value #' @export #' @examples #' example_function("example") example_function <- function(param1) {   # Function implementation } devtools::document()

title: Log ratio of Ancestral Variances
text: LAVA (Log ratio Ancestral Variances) R package designed help researchers analyze genomic data lens ancestral variance ratios. package provides tools statistical analysis visualization evolutionary patterns.

title: Developing LAVA in VS Code
text: guide provides instructions developing LAVA R package using Visual Studio Code, particularly working remote machine.

title: Developing LAVA in VS Code
text: Install following VS Code extensions: R Extension VS Code - Main R language support R Debugger - debugging R code radian - alternative R console

title: Developing LAVA in VS Code
text: Open VS Code settings (File > Preferences > Settings) Windows: Usually C:\\Program Files\\R\\R-4.x.x\\bin\\R.exe Mac: Usually /usr/local/bin/R /Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Resources/bin/R Linux: Usually /usr/bin/R

title: Developing LAVA in VS Code
text: better code intelligence: Install languageserver package R: VS Code settings, enable R language server

title: Developing LAVA in VS Code
text: working remote machine: Install Remote - SSH extension VS Code Connect remote server via SSH Open LAVA project folder remote machine VS Code use R installation remote machine

text: R executable found: Check r.rterm setting VS Code Language server working: Ensure languageserver package installed run code interactively: Check R terminal active

title: Developing LAVA in VS Code
text: Slow connection: Consider using VS Code’s workspace sync feature Git authentication: Set SSH keys seamless GitHub authentication File permission issues: Check file permissions remote machine
